settings.title: CX Designer Skill Matrix
settings.subtitle: Mark your proficiency level (1-8) for each skill

core.name: Customer journey mapping
core.explanation: Creating end-to-end visualizations of customer experiences across all touchpoints, channels, and stages of the relationship.

core.name: Voice of Customer programs
core.explanation: Designing and managing systematic approaches to capture, analyze, and act on customer feedback and insights.

core.name: CX metrics & measurement
core.explanation: Defining and tracking key experience indicators like NPS, CSAT, CES, and linking them to business outcomes.

core.name: Touchpoint analysis
core.explanation: Evaluating individual interaction points to identify friction, opportunities, and moments that matter most to customers.

core.name: Experience mapping
core.explanation: Visualizing the holistic customer experience including emotions, expectations, and perceptions at each stage.

core.name: Customer research methods
core.explanation: Planning and conducting qualitative and quantitative research to understand customer needs, behaviors, and pain points.

core.name: Data analysis & insights
core.explanation: Interpreting customer data from multiple sources to identify patterns, trends, and actionable opportunities.

core.name: Persona development
core.explanation: Creating evidence-based customer archetypes that represent key segments and guide experience design decisions.

core.name: Service recovery design
core.explanation: Designing processes and experiences to effectively handle failures and turn detractors into promoters.

core.name: Omnichannel experience design
core.explanation: Creating seamless, consistent experiences across all channels (digital, physical, human) throughout the customer lifecycle.

core.name: CX strategy development
core.explanation: Defining the vision, priorities, and roadmap for customer experience aligned with business objectives.

core.name: Feedback loop design
core.explanation: Creating systems to continuously capture customer input and close the loop with improvements and communication.

supporting.name: Stakeholder management
supporting.explanation: Building relationships and managing expectations with executives, teams, and partners across the organization.

supporting.name: Cross-functional collaboration
supporting.explanation: Working effectively with marketing, sales, operations, IT, and other teams to deliver unified customer experiences.

supporting.name: Strategic thinking
supporting.explanation: Connecting CX initiatives to business strategy and demonstrating ROI of experience improvements.

supporting.name: Storytelling & presentation
supporting.explanation: Communicating customer insights and CX vision compellingly to inspire action and secure buy-in.

supporting.name: Empathy
supporting.explanation: Deeply understanding and advocating for customer perspectives, emotions, and unmet needs.

supporting.name: Change management
supporting.explanation: Guiding organizations through CX transformation, addressing resistance, and building customer-centric culture.

supporting.name: Influencing & persuasion
supporting.explanation: Driving CX priorities and decisions without direct authority across siloed organizations.

supporting.name: Business acumen
supporting.explanation: Understanding business models, P&L impact, and translating CX improvements into financial outcomes.

supporting.name: Problem solving
supporting.explanation: Identifying root causes of experience issues and developing creative, feasible solutions.

supporting.name: Customer advocacy
supporting.explanation: Championing the customer perspective in organizational decisions and balancing it with business constraints.
